title: scalia arachnodea
side_effects: Scalia arachnodea can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing nausea and dizziness in individuals who consume it in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils can also lead to skin irritation, including rashes and itching. In some cases, improper use of Scalia arachnodea has been linked to liver damage and other gastrointestinal issues, particularly in individuals with pre-existing health conditions. Furthermore, inhaling the plant's vapors can cause respiratory problems, including asthma-like symptoms and coughing.
